content: We can frame LLM self-reflection mechanisms as a computational equivalent of the critical reflective learning practice taught in high English language arts curricula, building on the original Reflexion autonomous agent framework that first formalized self-reflection as a core agent capability. Just as students are taught to revisit their written drafts to catch logical gaps, rephrase awkward arguments, and correct misinterpretations of prompts through structured reflection, large language models can use graded reflective practice modeled after this educational pedagogy to iteratively improve their outputs.
